# hyperfluid_key_value_cache (Resource)

A managed Valkey key-value cache.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "hyperfluid_env" "default" {
  name = "default"
}

resource "hyperfluid_key_value_cache" "sessions" {
  env           = data.hyperfluid_env.default.id
  name             = "sessions"
  maxmemory        = "256mb"
  maxmemory_policy = "allkeys-lru"
}

# Connection credentials are exposed as a secret reference, not plaintext.
output "credentials_secret" {
  value = hyperfluid_key_value_cache.sessions.credentials_secret_name
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `env` (String) Environment id the cache runs in. Changing this forces a new cache.
- `name` (String) Cache name (RFC 1035 label). Changing this forces a new cache.

### Optional

- `description` (String) Free-form description.
- `image` (String) Valkey container image (defaults to a platform-pinned image).
- `maxmemory` (String) Valkey maxmemory, e.g. "256mb".
- `maxmemory_policy` (String) Valkey eviction policy, e.g. "allkeys-lru".
- `tags` (List of String) User-defined tags.

### Read-Only

- `credentials_secret_name` (String) Name of the secret holding the connection credentials (reference it with secret:<name>).
- `host` (String) Cache host, once provisioned.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `phase` (String) Current lifecycle phase.
- `port` (Number) Cache port, once provisioned.
- `slug` (String) Derived slug.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Key-value cache import id is the cache id (uuid).
terraform import hyperfluid_key_value_cache.sessions 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
```
